symptoms.symptom_name: Acute pounding, racing chest pain / tightness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute stress reaction / adjustment disorder with anxiety
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest tightness with palpitations is a common somatic feature of an acute autonomic/panic crisis.
Chest pain with diaphoresis mandates exclusion of ACS via ECG/troponin and in-person exam — not possible via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute stress reaction / adjustment disorder with anxiety
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adrenergic surge produces sweating in acute stress.
Diaphoresis accompanying chest pain raises the pre-test probability of an acute cardiac event.
Heat intolerance and sweating occur in thyrotoxicosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute situational working-memory failure with preserved alertness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute stress reaction / adjustment disorder with anxiety
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reversible working-memory reduction under high cognitive load/threat is characteristic; preserved alertness argues against a primary neurological event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptoms strictly bound to the evaluation trigger, absent at rest otherwise

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute stress reaction / adjustment disorder with anxiety
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tight, reproducible psychological trigger with no symptoms at rest points to a stress-precipitated aetiology.
Purely psychologically-triggered, non-exertional pattern lowers cardiac probability but does not exclude it remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of crushing/radiating exertional chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-radiating, non-crushing pain lowers but does not eliminate ACS risk — atypical MI presentations occur, especially in women.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute stress reaction / adjustment disorder with anxiety
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective autonomic hyperarousal — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers this from patient-reported racing, not a measured value.
A measured sustained rate requires ECG rhythm characterisation to exclude a primary arrhythmia.
An ECG is the key first-line test to exclude ischaemia and characterise the tachycardia; never available in real time via telehealth.
Rhythm diagnosis depends on the ECG.
A normal thyroid exam lowers the probability of thyrotoxicosis but TFTs remain confirmatory — sealed in-person finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cervical and thoracic paraspinal muscle tension

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute stress reaction / adjustment disorder with anxiety
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Muscular tension is a common somatic correlate of sustained stress — sealed in-person finding.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Evaluation-triggered acute autonomic crisis with cognitive blanking
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute stress reaction / adjustment disorder with anxiety
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these strongly suggest a stress-precipitated autonomic crisis — but only once the cardiac/thyroid mimics have been excluded in person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line test to exclude ischaemia and characterise the tachycardia; requires in-person/urgent-care attendance.
If any cardiac suspicion remains after ECG, troponin is required to exclude myocardial injury — obtained at urgent care / ED.
Excludes thyrotoxicosis as a cause of the tachycardia and diaphoresis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Neuropsychological assessment of situational working memory
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: For longer-term characterisation of the stress-related cognitive symptoms once acute causes are excluded — GP-arranged, not acute.
The AI must not prescribe a beta-blocker or sedative remotely before a cardiac cause is excluded; this is an error of commission, not an investigation.
